rulings.Case Title: JUAN SALAS ET AL VS. GALE LEA GRINSELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-21-593109, CGC-21-593044 WITH CASE CGC-21-588918
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 5, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT GALE GRINSELL, FOUNDERS REALTY, INC., RAYMOND GRINSELL MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-21-593109, CGC-21-593044 WITH CASE CGC-21-588918 is DENIED. The parties in each case may use discovery in all cases to limit duplication of efforts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 124 MASON STREET PROPERTY, LLC VS. SUSAN STEINKOENIG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Judgment And To Quash The Writ Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 5, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT SUSAN STEINKOENIG Notice Of Motion; Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Judgment And To Quash The Writ Of Possession is GRATNED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Quash Writ of Execution is GRANTED. The writ is based on a judgment terminating the tenancy and forfeiting the lease as of March 13, 2021. Thereafter, with knowledge of such termination and all the violations precipitating the filing of the unlawful detainer, Plaintiff accepted payments in exchange for possession. March 13, 2020 judgment cannot support a writ of possession and the relationship created after March 13, 2020 has not been terminated or adjudicated to not be a tenancy. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a new tenancy has not been created when payment was accepted in exchange for possession. Ramish v. Workman (1917) 33 Cal.App. 19 addressed the measure of holdover damages in a case filed subsequent to the owner obtaining a judgment for possession and before that judgment became final. Motion to Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Motion is untimely under CCP 473(b) and fails to show any mistake, surprise or excusable neglect that caused entry of judgment in March 2020. To the extent, Defendant appears to claim that the judgment is void (not stated as grounds for the motion in the Notice of Motion), moving party failed to establish that the judgment terminating a tenancy becomes void when a new tenancy is created. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
